# Development Skill

## Module Identification

Before creating code, determine the correct module:

- Check `docs/architecture/modules.md` for the module map
- Each domain entity gets its own feature module
- If no existing module owns the work, scaffold a new one

## Scaffolding Checklist

When creating a new feature module, create these files:

1. Module declaration file
2. Entry layer file (handles API dispatch only)
3. Service file (business logic, caching, data access)
4. Data model / schema file
5. DTO / input type files (create, update)
6. Unit test files (one per source file, co-located)

Follow the naming conventions defined in architecture instructions.

## Dependency Rules

- Entry layer → Service → Data model + Cache
- Never reverse this direction
- Never import between feature modules
- Inject global providers (config, cache) via constructor injection

## Validation

After implementation:

1. Run linter to check code style
2. Run tests to verify correctness
3. Verify no forbidden imports exist

## References

- `.github/instructions/architecture.instructions.md` — naming conventions, module structure, dependency rules
- `.github/instructions/testing.instructions.md` — test co-location, mocking patterns
